en: Construction - Confined Spaces
Before entry, hazards must be identified and assessed.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 3(5)0
Hazard Assessment
Confined space work notification must be issued for containers, pits, shafts, channels, or pipelines requiring written protective measures.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 3(5)1
Reporting to Labor Inspectorate

A written entry order must be issued by the supervisor before entry or work begins, specifying required protective measures and assigning continuous oversight responsibility.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 120(2)
Permit Entry Order (Pre-Work)

Entry must be authorized only after protective measures are confirmed to be implemented by the responsible person identified in the entry order.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 120(3)
Supervisor Entry Authorization
Atmospheric hazards must be ruled out before entry by verifying that no oxygen deficiency or hazardous substance concentrations are present.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 122(2)
Atmospheric Testing
Fresh air introduction and adequate ventilation must be provided, and oxygen must not be used for ventilation purposes.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 122(1)
Adequate Ventilation

Protective measures for entry into containers, silos, shafts, pits, trenches, ditches, canals, and pipelines must include ventilation, atmospheric hazard controls, supervision, entry permission, rescue capability, and appropriate personal protective equipment.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 120(2-7), 122(1-6)
Control Measures (Engineering, Administrative, and/or Personal Protective Equipment)
A trained attendant must remain at the entry point throughout operations and be familiar with the work and rescue measures.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 122(3)
Attendant at Entry

Reliable technical monitoring (e.g., radio/telephone) must be used when visual contact or verbal communication with the entrant is not possible.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 122(3)
Communication System

The entrant must be secured against falls, drowning, and sinking using suitable personal protective equipment (PPE), with the rope end secured outside and slack minimized. Suitable rescue equipment (e.g., winch/hoist) must be provided when rescue from above may be necessary.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 122(4, 6)
Emergency Rescue Arrangements
